oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Comparison of tulathromycin versus enrofloxacin for the treatment of bovine respiratory disease following metaphylaxis in U.S. commercial feedlots
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Terrell, Shane
Lowe, James
Jobman, Erin
Thomson, Dan
Newberry, Jessica
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: Macrolide antimicrobial drugs are commonly used for treat­ment and control of bovine respiratory disease (BRD). The objective of this study was to compare the efficacy of tulath­romycin and enrofloxacin for treatment of BRD, following metaphylaxis with tulathromycin.
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: American Association of Bovine Practitioners Conference Proceedings; Vol. 58 No. 2 (2025): 2025 AABP Annual Conference Proceedings; 230
oai_dc:dc.dc:rights: Copyright (c) 2026 American Association of Bovine Practitioners
